core_mechanism: operator factorization / intertwining (Darboux) transformation: expressing a self-adjoint operator as A†A and its partner as AA†, which forces the two operators to share an identical spectrum apart from their kernel (zero-mode) states, whose net count is a topological index
brief_reason: The abstract builds pairs of isospectral potentials that differ only in whether they support zero modes and measures those zero modes with the Witten index, which is exactly the A†A / AA† factorization producing spectrally identical partners distinguished only by kernel states counted by an index.
